title: Building Dashboards in Sheets, CRM, or Metabase

questions.question: According to the 5-Metric Rule, what should you do if a dashboard metric doesn't change your next action?

options.text: Move it to a secondary dashboard
Track it monthly instead of weekly

questions.explanation: If you can't explain why a metric changes your next action, delete it. Your dashboard should have 5-7 metrics maximum. Pattern recognition requires seeing all metrics simultaneously without noise.

questions.question: What is the recommended dashboard tool for a solo founder managing fewer than 50 deals per month?
questions.explanation: With under 50 deals/month, Google Sheets is the best choice. Manual data entry takes only 10-15 minutes weekly, it gives you full control, costs nothing, and is easy to share. Automate later if you scale.

questions.question: Why should a Google Sheets dashboard use a 5-tab structure rather than putting everything on one tab?

options.text: Google Sheets has a row limit per tab
Separation of concerns prevents accidental overwrites and makes debugging easier

questions.explanation: Separating raw data, calculations, dashboard view, historical archive, and config prevents accidental formula overwrites and makes debugging easier. Each tab has a clear purpose.

questions.question: Your dashboard should show only the current week's numbers without historical trends.
questions.explanation: False. Every metric should show the current value, the trend (last 4-8 weeks), and a benchmark or target. Without historical tracking, you can't identify trends or measure improvement.

questions.question: Choose which dashboard tool you will use (Sheets, CRM, or Metabase) and explain why it matches your deal volume, technical comfort, and budget. What 5 metrics will you track on your dashboard?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response selects a specific tool with a clear rationale tied to their deal volume, technical skills, and budget. Check that they list approximately 5 specific, actionable metrics (not vanity metrics) and explain why each one drives a decision.
